title: Direct ELISA for investigating the binding of Protein-A  to immunoglobulins.
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"TvSm8","text":"MATERIALS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":null,"data":null},{"key":"CTqYr","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null},{"key":"KHIGG","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":220123,"guid":"41CA39BE341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Nunc\u0026trade; 96-Well Polystyrene Round Bottom Microwell Plates, V 96 well plate, Non-Treated, clear, without lid, Sterile","mol_weight":0,"sku":"260210","url":"https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/260210","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":440,"name":"Thermo Fisher","link":"https://www.thermofisher.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/440.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/440.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}},"1":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":557162,"guid":"425BF391341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Staphylococcal Protein-A","mol_weight":0,"sku":"","url":"","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":17,"name":"Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich)","link":"http://sigmaaldrich.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: This ELISA is used to study the interaction of protein-A (SpA) with diverse immunoglobulins.
The 96 well microtitre plate is coated overnight at 4°C with 1 µg/µl per well of purified immunoglobulins or 50 µl of any animal sera in carbonate-bicarbonate buffer pH 9.6.
Then plate is treated with bovine serum albumin solution and washed 4X with PBS-Tween.
Then 50 µl of peroxidase-labeled-protein-A conjugate diluted 1:5000 in PBS-non-fat milk is added to each well and incubated for 1.30h at RT. After that the plate is washed 4X with PBS-Tween.
Pipette 50 μl of 3,3',5,5' - tetramethylbenzidine (TMB; Sigma-Aldrich) to each well.
The reaction is stopped with 50 µl of 3M H2SO4 solution.
The plate is visually assessed for the development of colour and read in a microplate reader at 450 nm.
A cut-off point should be calculated as the mean of the optical density of negative controls x 2 SD.
creator: Angel A Justiz-Vaillant
